failure_modes.text: Teams can celebrate novelty while failing to prove the replacement is more useful or executable.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require proof that the replacement is more useful or executable than the incumbent before celebrating novelty.

failure_modes.text: False pattern recognition can protect the old frame because it still feels familiar and previously successful.
failure_modes.source_quote: I have seen this one before, it is an X problem
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge the old frame explicitly: name the assumption that has changed, not just the feeling that something new is needed.

failure_modes.text: Ignoring the human dimension turns rational reinvention slides into failed implementation.
failure_modes.source_quote: implementation is treated purely as a fundamentally rational process
failure_modes.mitigation: Account for the human dimension: map stakeholder resistance, retraining needs, and transition costs alongside the rational case for reinvention.

premortem_questions.text: What would need to be true for this supposedly disruptive replacement to actually work better than the incumbent logic?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.text: Which prior commitment is blocking us from killing a wrong idea quickly enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: public prior strategy blocks timely reinvention

premortem_questions.text: Are we replacing obsolete logic or just performing boldness for ourselves?
premortem_questions.source_quote: clear evidence that the replacement is more truthful

heuristics.text: Get really good at destroying your own wrong ideas early so evidence can redirect the work.
heuristics.source_quote: really good at destroying your own wrong ideas

heuristics.text: Do not boil the ocean; gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the hypothesis.
Use what-you-have-to-believe analysis to make reinvention assumptions concrete before investing behind them.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
